unit_09 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_09

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 331 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
While catalogs of record labels that specialize in Native American music are useful indices of Native American consumers' preferences, their (i)______ are also (ii)______ : some genres of Native American music have been deemed inappropriate to record because of their special cultural significance.While catalogs of record labels that specialize in Native American music are useful indices of Native American consumers' preferences, their (i)______ are also (ii)______ : some genres of Native American music have been deemed inappropriate to record because of their special cultural significance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　omissions; telling
deemgenrerecord
deem: standard unit headword | genre: standard unit headword | record: standard unit headword
#2 Sentence Equivalence
In a field of egotists, Bloomfield is ______, always praising her competitors and punctuating her correspondence with self-deprecating remarks.In a field of egotists, Bloomfield is ______, always praising her competitors and punctuating her correspondence with self-deprecating remarks.
答案：A, F　unassuming; modest
punctuateunassumingmodest
punctuate: standard unit headword | unassuming: standard unit headword | modest: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) about science.Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) about science.Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　expansion; distrust; grudging
indeedparallel
indeed: standard unit headword | parallel: standard unit headword

答案：A, B　indifference; apathy
receivedassume
received: standard unit headword | assume: standard unit headword
#5 Text Completion
The author affects ______ in the tone of his novels that is quite at odds with his predilection for invective in his nonfiction publications.The author affects ______ in the tone of his novels that is quite at odds with his predilection for invective in his nonfiction publications.
答案：B　a neutrality
predilectionpublication
predilection: standard unit headword | publication: standard unit headword
#6 SE
Nicolaus Copernicus' On the Revolutions of the Celestial Spheres, published in 1543, laid out the theory that Earth and the other planets go around the Sun, __________ a long-held belief that Earth was the center of the universe.Nicolaus Copernicus' On the Revolutions of the Celestial Spheres, published in 1543, laid out the theory that Earth and the other planets go around the Sun, __________ a long-held belief that Earth was the center of the universe.
答案：A, E　contravening; gainsaying
contraveneconsolidate
contravene: standard unit headword | consolidate: standard unit headword
#7 Sentence Equivalence
The territory’s tradition of simple and low taxes, combined with a comparatively easygoing government, has earned it the ______ of its citizens and is widely seen as a main reason for its stunning rise to prosperity.The territory’s tradition of simple and low taxes, combined with a comparatively easygoing government, has earned it the ______ of its citizens and is widely seen as a main reason for its stunning rise to prosperity.
答案：E, F　approbation; adulation
stunstunning
stun: standard unit headword | stunning: standard unit headword
#8 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
insidiousemploy
insidious: standard unit headword | employ: standard unit headword
#9 Text Completion
Industry-sponsored scientific research on chemical safety often (i)______. Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)______ that research. Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to (iii)______ the credibility of scientific work.Industry-sponsored scientific research on chemical safety often (i)______. Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)______ that research. Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to (iii)______ the credibility of scientific work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　elicits skepticism; invalidate; vitiate
elicitsuffice
elicit: standard unit headword | suffice: standard unit headword

The jazz style called bebop was born and nurtured in New York City, and despite a ______ initial reception, it resonated three thousand miles away on the West Coast.The jazz style called bebop was born and nurtured in New York City, and despite a ______ initial reception, it resonated three thousand miles away on the West Coast.
答案：C, D　confused; muddled
receptionnurture

#11 Text Completion
A transformative scientific idea that emerged in the eighteenth century was the realization that slow, inexorable geological processes follow the basic laws of physics and chemistry. This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.A transformative scientific idea that emerged in the eighteenth century was the realization that slow, inexorable geological processes follow the basic laws of physics and chemistry. This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　an obvious; followed; revolutionary for
inexorablehindsight
inexorable: standard unit headword | hindsight: standard unit headword

Blank 2

It is troubling that blogs, which may be among the least reliable sources of information in human history, occupy such ______.-It is troubling that blogs, which may be among the least reliable sources of information in human history, occupy such ______.-
答案：B, F　conspicuous; prominent
enviabletrouble
enviable: standard unit headword | trouble: standard unit headword
#14 Text Completion
Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very ______ of what science is supposed to be.Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very ______ of what science is supposed to be.
答案：E　antithesis
exemplarmedieval
exemplar: standard unit headword | medieval: standard unit headword

In a conspicuous attempt to illustrate the _________ of her aesthetic discernment, she recounted how, during her childhood, she had often entertained adults with her sophisticated views on art.In a conspicuous attempt to illustrate the _________ of her aesthetic discernment, she recounted how, during her childhood, she had often entertained adults with her sophisticated views on art.
答案：B　precocity
discernment

To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　question the adequacy; conducive to
conducive
conducive: standard unit headword
#17 TC SINGLE
Natural selection has favored some instinctive reactions of organisms over others, so only those that promote survival and reproduction have become established, but instinctive responses need not be ____________: they may adjust over time.Natural selection has favored some instinctive reactions of organisms over others, so only those that promote survival and reproduction have become established, but instinctive responses need not be ____________: they may adjust over time.
答案：B　immutable
instinctive
instinctive: standard unit headword
#18 TC DOUBLE
Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　indirect; an inference
vaporize
vaporize: standard unit headword
#19 SE
In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.
答案：B, D　haphazard; arbitrary
haphazard

Although often seen as simply an inefficiency, redundancy in language actually serves as the (i) __________ to (ii) __________. It provides second chances, in other words. Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.Although often seen as simply an inefficiency, redundancy in language actually serves as the (i) __________ to (ii) __________. It provides second chances, in other words. Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C　capricious
decision-making

Blank 2

Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. Blank 3

With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　innate; harmonizes with; adapt
harmonize

What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. Blank 2
Blank 3

#48 Text Completion

But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
budding
budding: standard unit headword
#49 Sentence Equivalence
Science is arguably a very high-minded pursuit, but that is not to say that all of its practitioners are ______, as numerous articles alleging overly generous pharmaceutical industry payments have tried to show.Science is arguably a very high-minded pursuit, but that is not to say that all of its practitioners are ______, as numerous articles alleging overly generous pharmaceutical industry payments have tried to show.
答案：C, E　unimpeachable; blameless
unimpeachable
unimpeachable: standard unit headword

Catherine the Great comes across in her memoirs as (i)______ ruler with a razor-sharp intellect, letting nothing stand in the way of her ambitions. In short, the impression the memoirs give is entirely in accord with her reputation for being (ii)______.Catherine the Great comes across in her memoirs as (i)______ ruler with a razor-sharp intellect, letting nothing stand in the way of her ambitions. In short, the impression the memoirs give is entirely in accord with her reputation for being (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　an uncompromising; formidable
razor-sharp

答案：B, D　intelligible; readable
heartfelt

But this agricultural archive is (i)______ as a result of war, storms, scant money, and bad management, particularly in the world’s most (ii)______ places.There are something like 1,400 seed banks around the world, which guard samples of crop plants ranging from alfalfa to yams. But this agricultural archive is (i)______ as a result of war, storms, scant money, and bad management, particularly in the world’s most (ii)______ places.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　eroding; turbulent

答案：A, E　limpid; pellucid

distill: standard unit headword

In truth, the preservation of the reef (iii)______, a combination of active government intervention and the beneficial effects of responsible tourism.(i)______ have often shrilled that Australia’s Great Barrier Reef is dying, a result of agricultural runoff from the (ii)______ Queensland coast. In truth, the preservation of the reef (iii)______, a combination of active government intervention and the beneficial effects of responsible tourism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　Scaremongers; over-farmed; is something of a success story

答案：C, E　a prophylactic; an inhibitory

答案：C, F　burgeoning; expansion

Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　rich; problem; augmented

tease: standard unit headword
#58 Text Completion
The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)______, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)______, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly

答案：B, D　sleazy; sordid

答案：A　significance

But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. 答案：D, F　a puzzle; a conundrum
recordmedieval
record: standard unit headword | medieval: standard unit headword

The writer assumes that most people feel some version of vexed ambivalence toward large corporations: __________ the convenience they deliver yet resentful of the trade-offs that they continually demand.The writer assumes that most people feel some version of vexed ambivalence toward large corporations: __________ the convenience they deliver yet resentful of the trade-offs that they continually demand.
答案：B, F　enthralled by; dazzled by
assume
assume: standard unit headword
#152 Text Completion
The contemporary trend whereby fashion designers flout mainstream tradition is unique only in its (i)______; earlier fashion designers experienced the same (ii)______ impulse, albeit in a less extreme form.The contemporary trend whereby fashion designers flout mainstream tradition is unique only in its (i)______; earlier fashion designers experienced the same (ii)______ impulse, albeit in a less extreme form.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　intensity; iconoclastic
iconoclastic
iconoclastic: standard unit headword
#153 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt’s book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i)_______ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii)_______ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii)_______ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt’s book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i)_______ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii)_______ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii)_______ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror
champion
champion: standard unit headword
#154 Sentence Equivalence
Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not ______ his moderate political record.Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not ______ his moderate political record.
答案：B, F　conform to; square with
recordradical
record: standard unit headword | radical: standard unit headword
#155 SE
Owls are a staple of children's books and cultural kitsch; yet, while it might therefore be assumed that we have a certain __________ owls, only lately have scientists begun to understand the birds in any detail.Owls are a staple of children's books and cultural kitsch; yet, while it might therefore be assumed that we have a certain __________ owls, only lately have scientists begun to understand the birds in any detail.
答案：C, D　familiarity with; acquaintance with
assume
assume: standard unit headword

Because people expect theater directors to be authoritarians, many were surprised that Clark was so ______.Because people expect theater directors to be authoritarians, many were surprised that Clark was so ______.
答案：C　egalitarian
egalitarian
egalitarian: standard unit headword
#157 TC TRIPLE
The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained
expedient
expedient: standard unit headword
#158 Sentence Equivalence
The stories of silent drama may often have been ______, yet, within those basic outlines, the true artists among silent-film actors could express shadings that had no immediate analogue in language.The stories of silent drama may often have been ______, yet, within those basic outlines, the true artists among silent-film actors could express shadings that had no immediate analogue in language.
答案：D, E　elementary; rudimentary
analogue
analogue: standard unit headword
#159 Text Completion
One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______ , exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes, which is easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______ , exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes, which is easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　innate; harmonizes with; adapt
harmonize
harmonize: standard unit headword
#160 SE
The Czech surrealist artist Toyen remains something of __________ in the art world despite the publication of an important monograph on her work in conjunction with a major retrospective exhibition.The Czech surrealist artist Toyen remains something of __________ in the art world despite the publication of an important monograph on her work in conjunction with a major retrospective exhibition.
答案：A, E　an enigma; a mystery
publication
publication: standard unit headword
#161 Sentence Equivalence
Some kinds of deadly bacteria, including those that cause tetanus, tuberculosis, and botulism, remain ______ until something triggers their insidious activity.Some kinds of deadly bacteria, including those that cause tetanus, tuberculosis, and botulism, remain ______ until something triggers their insidious activity.
答案：A, B　harmless; innocuous
insidious
insidious: standard unit headword
#162 Sentence Equivalence
Although one can adduce myriad examples of ecosystem disruption by non-indigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survive in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystems they have invaded.Although one can adduce myriad examples of ecosystem disruption by non-indigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survive in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystems they have invaded.
答案：A, B　minimal; trifling
trifling
trifling: standard unit headword
#163 SE
At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears __________.At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears __________.
答案：A, D　blithe; winsome
modest
modest: standard unit headword
#164 Text Completion
A transformative scientific idea that emerged in the eighteenth century was the realization that slow, inexorable geological processes follow the basic laws of physics and chemistry. This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.A transformative scientific idea that emerged in the eighteenth century was the realization that slow, inexorable geological processes follow the basic laws of physics and chemistry. This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　an obvious; followed; revolutionary for
inexorablehindsight
inexorable: standard unit headword | hindsight: standard unit headword

Although the virtues of supplementing wild fish populations with fish from hatcheries are often_______, scientific studies have indicated that fish stocking can harm the wild, native species of the water body.Although the virtues of supplementing wild fish populations with fish from hatcheries are often_______, scientific studies have indicated that fish stocking can harm the wild, native species of the water body.
答案：B, E　extolled; acclaimed
contest
contest: standard unit headword
#166 Text Completion
The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)______ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)______ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deferential; radical; profound shift
radical
radical: standard unit headword

When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

答案：E　inevitable

One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. #202 Text Completion
Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook
insufficient
insufficient: standard unit headword
#203 Sentence Equivalence
As a historical genre, biography is best when ______, a careful reconstruction of the past in all its unfamiliar particularity.As a historical genre, biography is best when ______, a careful reconstruction of the past in all its unfamiliar particularity.
答案：E, F　meticulous; thorough
genre
genre: standard unit headword
#204 Text Completion
For the urban researcher, the long lives of ancient cities can provide ample chronological data, making up for the paucity stemming from relative ______ of most present-day cities.For the urban researcher, the long lives of ancient cities can provide ample chronological data, making up for the paucity stemming from relative ______ of most present-day cities.
答案：E　youthfulness
ample
ample: standard unit headword
#205 Sentence Equivalence
The current ______ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.The current ______ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.
答案：A, D　glut; surfeit
record
record: standard unit headword
#206 Text Completion
The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood
indeed
indeed: standard unit headword
#207 Sentence Equivalence
Despite their cultural and social significance, rapid growth, and widespread appeal in China, video games—unlike traditional media—have received ______ attention from international communication researchers.Despite their cultural and social significance, rapid growth, and widespread appeal in China, video games—unlike traditional media—have received ______ attention from international communication researchers.
答案：B, E　scant; limited
received
received: standard unit headword
#208 Text Completion
A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i)______, meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii)______.A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i)______, meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　disingenuous; high-minded purpose
radical
radical: standard unit headword
#209 Sentence Equivalence
Although the essayist’s arguments did not ______ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.Although the essayist’s arguments did not ______ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.
答案：B, E　confound; perplex
misinterpret
misinterpret: standard unit headword
#210 Text Completion
The often-cited parallels between human communities and insect colonies are ______: the cooperation found among social insects is essentially due to the insect’s genetic ties, while humans often collaborate with non-relatives.The often-cited parallels between human communities and insect colonies are ______: the cooperation found among social insects is essentially due to the insect’s genetic ties, while humans often collaborate with non-relatives.
答案：A　superficial

答案：B, D　hindered; inhibited

#212 Text Completion
The nature of our recollection is (i)______ . One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollection is (i)______ . One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

record: standard unit headword
#213 Sentence Equivalence
Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of ______ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of ______ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.
答案：D, E　solidarity; camaraderie
elicit
elicit: standard unit headword
#214 Text Completion
Some recent research shows that people tend to (i)______ advice from (ii)______ source, even to the point that we are willing to forgive an adviser's poor track record. This can drive those offering advice to (iii)______ and bodes ill for advisers who try to be honest about gaps in their knowledge.Some recent research shows that people tend to (i)______ advice from (ii)______ source, even to the point that we are willing to forgive an adviser's poor track record. This can drive those offering advice to (iii)______ and bodes ill for advisers who try to be honest about gaps in their knowledge.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　trust; a confident; exaggerate their certainty

Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to ______ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to ______ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.
答案：A, E　hamstring; impair
ample
ample: standard unit headword
#216 Text Completion
Although Thaler employs an innovative mode of analysis, his study offers yet another examination of quite (i)______ ground—namely, the cultural ideology of Norwegian-American preservationist writers in the early twentieth century. The history, literature, and changing internal dynamics of the Norwegian subculture in America constitute a particularly well-studied area. Anyone familiar with the authoritative work of scholars in the field will (ii)______ little in Thaler’s study that is (iii)______.Although Thaler employs an innovative mode of analysis, his study offers yet another examination of quite (i)______ ground—namely, the cultural ideology of Norwegian-American preservationist writers in the early twentieth century. The history, literature, and changing internal dynamics of the Norwegian subculture in America constitute a particularly well-studied area. Anyone familiar with the authoritative work of scholars in the field will (ii)______ little in Thaler’s study that is (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　well-worked; find; new

employ: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic

#218 Text Completion
With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)______ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)______ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk
indeed
indeed: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　snag; hitch
champion
champion: standard unit headword
#220 Text Completion
Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; A staleness
publication
publication: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　pacific; dovish

Just as large manufacturing companies with dominant positions in large domestic markets were once able to resist (i)______ despite ample signs that foreign competition was rapidly overtaking them, strong and wealthy states can (ii)______ and still manage to limp along for many years.Just as large manufacturing companies with dominant positions in large domestic markets were once able to resist (i)______ despite ample signs that foreign competition was rapidly overtaking them, strong and wealthy states can (ii)______ and still manage to limp along for many years.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　innovation; maintain misguided policies

答案：D, E　alarm; unsettle

He was a (ii)______ figure in the Middle East Studies Association of North America, editing for five years its flagship publication, and in 2004 he was even elected the association’s incoming president. But because his research focused on highly (iii)______ aspects of the eighteenth-and-nineteenth century Middle East, he was unlikely to achieve any sort of public acclaim.For many years Cole experienced (i)______ his professional circle. He was a (ii)______ figure in the Middle East Studies Association of North America, editing for five years its flagship publication, and in 2004 he was even elected the association’s incoming president. But because his research focused on highly (iii)______ aspects of the eighteenth-and-nineteenth century Middle East, he was unlikely to achieve any sort of public acclaim.

For almost two centuries, the German island of Sylt has offered various therapies for every conceivable (i)______, from broken bones to liver complaints. The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)______ by the German medical system, which (iii)______ some of these treatments. Consequently, these treatments are widely used.For almost two centuries, the German island of Sylt has offered various therapies for every conceivable (i)______, from broken bones to liver complaints. The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)______ by the German medical system, which (iii)______ some of these treatments. Consequently, these treatments are widely used.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　malady; healthful; funds

#232 Text Completion
Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask
assume
assume: standard unit headword

There are many ways in which rat brains and human brains are ______; indeed, rat brains are often used as generalized models for all mammalian brains, including our own.There are many ways in which rat brains and human brains are ______; indeed, rat brains are often used as generalized models for all mammalian brains, including our own.
答案：B, D　analogous; comparable
indeed
indeed: standard unit headword

The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility

答案：C, F　uncertainty; instability

This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.

One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

#258 Text Completion
For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility
record
record: standard unit headword

Sports stars are often ______ figures, regarded as representative of the city or country for which they compete.Sports stars are often ______ figures, regarded as representative of the city or country for which they compete.
答案：A, F　totemic; emblematic

iconoclastic: standard unit headword
#260 Text Completion
The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　spontaneity; immediacy; improvisation
indeed
indeed: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　wayward; errant

transparent: standard unit headword

An affinity for (i)______ is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. But this predilection for (ii)______ can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.An affinity for (i)______ is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. But this predilection for (ii)______ can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.

Blank 2
答案：A, F　paradox; unresolvable ambivalences

答案：A, D　determines; governs

The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity

Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for

Behavior dubbed reprehensible by the residents of the region is considered conventional, even ______ by those of the neighboring region; fortunately, people traveling between the two are resigned to this disparity.Behavior dubbed reprehensible by the residents of the region is considered conventional, even ______ by those of the neighboring region; fortunately, people traveling between the two are resigned to this disparity.
答案：B　compulsory

#304 Text Completion
The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early 19th century, but it did not have (i)______ debut: many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)______, however, so the form (iii)______.The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early 19th century, but it did not have (i)______ debut: many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)______, however, so the form (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a propitious; practical alternatives; persisted

#305 Text Completion
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned

#306 Text Completion
Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)______ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)______ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)______ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)______ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands

#309 Text Completion
Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it

discern: standard unit headword
#310 Text Completion
By the early nineteenth century, education in the United States had become ______ affair: almost every town provided free schools for young children, and many were in the process of building high schools.By the early nineteenth century, education in the United States had become ______ affair: almost every town provided free schools for young children, and many were in the process of building high schools.
答案：B　a civic

#315 Text Completion
Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)______.Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　supplies; strong bargaining positions

Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

#323 Text Completion
The traditional set of nine planets could have been retained by defining a planet as any object that has the unique combination of characteristics of any one of the nine objects that were considered planets in the late twentieth century. That definition would not be (i)______ ; that is, it does not contradict the evidence. Its (ii)______ , however, renders it less (iii)______ than definitions based on astronomical criteria, such as orbit, size, and gravitation.The traditional set of nine planets could have been retained by defining a planet as any object that has the unique combination of characteristics of any one of the nine objects that were considered planets in the late twentieth century. That definition would not be (i)______ ; that is, it does not contradict the evidence. Its (ii)______ , however, renders it less (iii)______ than definitions based on astronomical criteria, such as orbit, size, and gravitation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　false; arbitrariness; defensible
expedient
expedient: standard unit headword
#324 Text Completion
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______ : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______ : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for
removed
removed: standard unit headword
#325 Text Completion
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique
indeedparallel
indeed: standard unit headword | parallel: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：B, D　inevitable; condemns

#327 Text Completion
Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unimpressed with; terrifying; a commonplace
